semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, eight source notes and their inventory/Appendix H boundaries, all 25 Lean declarations grouped under four manifest proof targets, the 4-valid/6-invalid intake corpus, the 2-valid/7-invalid re-contract corpus, the 3-valid/10-invalid plan-execution corpus, every prose scanner candidate, diagrams, objections, test plan, and downstream handoffs. The sweep replaced prompt-as-intent shorthand with a versioned interpretation contract; promoted raw-request separation, field disposition, bounded-default limits, affected-party authority, consumer binding, material-change re-contracting, preference non-authority, correction and appeal, and clarification-cost obligations into manifest ownership; separated formal record routing from natural-language understanding, consent, preference recovery, satisfaction, runtime authority, and effect safety; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared natural-language requests and their bounded interpretation, clarification, acceptance, re-contract, rejection, planning, evidence, preference-feedback, and downstream-consumer records, including ambiguous, emotional, urgent, multilingual, accessibility-sensitive, multi-party, privacy-sensitive, publication, spending, deployment, and affected-party cases.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack schemas, fixtures, finite Lean routes, and future public-safe natural or adversarial workloads with frozen request provenance, language, interface, model, prompt, retrieval, tools, policy, authority, affected parties, consumer, evaluator, and cost rules; real legal consent, clinical capacity, organizational authority, classified data, and open-world enforcement remain outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned interpretation-contract and receipt state machine whose current implementation validates declared record shape and finite routes; future interpretation claims require actual language models, independent authority/effect adjudication, direct-confirmation and requirements-engineering baselines, user correction, and downstream preservation tests.
scope.authority: The requester may propose outcomes and constraints only within authority they actually hold; affected-party rights and external policy remain separately owned; Constitutional Alignment may narrow or refuse; Command Contracts and Planning consume an exact accepted version; Runtime and Readiness require separate grants; Evidence States owns support transitions; Policy Optimization requires a separate update lease.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; request, contract, authority, affected-party, policy, consumer, source/privacy/publication, acceptance, model, prompt, evaluator, and expiry boundaries must be frozen for outcome-bearing campaigns and re-contracted after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, eight source notes, intent schema and fixtures, intake/re-contract/plan-execution records, Lean module, validator output, contract and receipt digests, campaign artifacts, evidence transitions, and public projections.

assumptions: Material outcome, means, authority, affected-party, source, privacy, publication, evidence, stop, expiry, consumer, and correction fields can be represented without treating the representation as complete inner intent.
Low-risk reversible planning defaults can be prospectively distinguished from authority-changing, irreversible, privacy-sensitive, publication, deployment, spending, and affected-party assumptions.

Independent adjudication can define bounded interpretation, unauthorized-action, missed-help, clarification-burden, correction, appeal, preservation, latency, privacy, and useful-throughput outcomes without using contract completeness as ground truth.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary dialogue, confirmations, goal-oriented requirements engineering, preference learning, policy checks, and runtime permissions already handle intent adequately; a formal interpretation contract adds friction, false precision, and stale paperwork while still failing to recover true preferences, consent, or authority.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable admitted trace widens means or authority, loses a material field, accepts a prohibited default, bypasses re-contracting, or uses raw text or inferred preference as effect authority; or a frozen simpler workflow matches or dominates the proposed contract on interpretation, unauthorized action, missed help, correction, burden, latency, privacy, and useful throughput across the declared transfer set.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze requirements-engineering, cooperative-inference, preference-feedback, prompt-structure, planning, constitutional, and authority comparators without claiming any source establishes the proposed control plane.
Justify authority ceilings, affected-party boundaries, default limits, privacy/publication constraints, correction, appeal, expiry, refusal, and acceptable clarification burden.
Specify finite preservation, no-authority, re-contract, consumer, expiry, rejection, and route invariants while exposing assumption-restating limits.
Exercise every schema field and downstream consumer with valid traces and exact rejecting mutations, including raw-context override, stale reuse, preference laundering, and constraint erosion.

Measure interpretation accuracy, unauthorized action, missed help, clarification burden, re-contract precision/recall, correction and appeal success, affected-party protection, latency, privacy, and useful throughput on held-out natural requests.

Ablate raw-request separation, field provenance, bounded defaults, authority checks, affected-party checks, consumer binding, re-contract triggers, correction, and least-authority routing under matched requests.

Repeat across languages, cultures, accessibility needs, emotional and urgent language, ambiguity, multi-party conflicts, preference drift, risk tiers, model families, planners, runtimes, and independently implemented evaluators.

contrary_evidence: Natural-language dialogue and direct confirmation can resolve many ambiguities cheaply, while rigid schemas can increase abandonment, workarounds, and false refusal.

Goal-oriented requirements engineering represents stakeholder goals and conflicts but does not itself authenticate authority, consent, or downstream permission; the current comparator note is not a reproduced method.
Cooperative inverse reinforcement learning and preference feedback formalize uncertainty or learn comparisons, but inferred rewards remain task- and model-dependent and are not authority grants.
A complete contract can encode a mistaken interpretation, manipulated confirmation, false stakeholder set, stale authority record, or coerced approval.
Least-authority defaults can miss helpful actions, create clarification fatigue, and burden users least able to express formal constraints.

Current evidence is internally authored schema validation, deterministic synthetic fixtures, and finite routing; it contains no natural-language evaluation, user or consent study, independent authority adjudication, or deployed effect test.
known_confounds: Annotator disagreement, cultural and linguistic variation, accessibility needs, emotion, urgency, power asymmetry, and interface framing can change apparent intent independently of contract quality.
The same model, prompt, author, policy, or evaluator may generate and judge the interpretation, creating correlated confirmation and design leakage.
Request selection, synthetic phrasing, reused probes, prompt tuning, hidden instructions, retrieval, and contamination can inflate interpretation or injection-resistance results.
Authority, consent, affected-party identity, legal capacity, organizational role, and resource ownership may be unobservable or change after acceptance.
Clarification can improve safety metrics by dropping difficult cases while reducing coverage and useful throughput; denominators must remain visible.
Downstream consumers can preserve digests while semantically eroding constraints or acting under a different lease.
Preference drift, revocation, expiry, policy, or consumer changes can invalidate an accepted contract even when its bytes remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Every material field has provenance and an exercised consumer; every seeded authority, affected-party, default, ambiguity, preservation, consumer, expiry, revocation, raw-context, preference, support, and material-change violation reaches its exact non-effect route.

A frozen natural and adversarial campaign meets joint interpretation, unauthorized-action, missed-help, clarification-burden, re-contract, correction, appeal, affected-party, latency, privacy, useful-throughput, uncertainty, and residual gates against strong comparators with an independent evaluator.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact request cohorts, languages, cultures, accessibility settings, risk tiers, authorities, affected parties, models, prompts, interfaces, policies, consumers, expiry windows, evaluators, and cost rules.

Separate schema validity, finite routing, language interpretation, preference prediction, authority authentication, informed consent, affected-party authorization, downstream preservation, runtime enforcement, satisfaction, support movement, and effect safety.
Unresolved authority, consent, affected parties, or material ambiguity retains draft, clarification, authority-pending, rejected, or residual status.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it or a required field has no exercised consumer.
Refute an interpretation benefit when matched ablation fails its frozen effect, uncertainty, coverage, correction, and cost rule or disappears under independent evaluation.
Refute superiority or generality when a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint frontier or the effect fails transfer.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise canonical owner supersedes the atom and all manifest, chapter, schema, proof, fixture, campaign, transition, appendix, public, and consumer references migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze a natural and adversarial request corpus, multilingual and affected-party cohorts, actual model interpreters, strong direct-confirmation/requirements/preference baselines, independent authority and effect adjudication, downstream planners and runtimes, exact joint metrics, correction/appeal routes, and transfer campaigns after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, contract completeness, citation, a green validator, or a theorem does not establish correct interpretation, true preference, informed consent, valid authority, satisfaction, downstream preservation, or safe effects.
The current 25 declarations and 35 synthetic fixture outcomes establish only bounded record and route consequences; they execute no language understanding or real-world authorization.
No support state, training authorization, publication permission, release readiness, action authority, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate gate and accepted transition.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.core.strongest_counterclaim: A formal contract relocates tacit ambiguity into a cumbersome document and still cannot capture consent or authority.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.core.falsifier: Any required field lacks a consumer, an accepted trace widens authority or loses a constraint, or a simpler workflow dominates the joint outcome-cost envelope.
acceptance_criteria: All hard boundaries reject and the full plane meets frozen interpretation, authority, correction, cost, and transfer gates.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.problem.0.strongest_counterclaim: Competent dialogue already distinguishes requests from permissions through clarification and runtime access control.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.problem.0.falsifier: Strong existing workflows lower representative requests with no material authority or preservation gap repaired here.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit identifies an unresolved request-to-authority failure without implying this remedy works.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.insuffici.strongest_counterclaim: Combined requirements, preference inference, confirmation, and permissions supply every named field at lower burden.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.insuffici.falsifier: A strong comparator authenticates all named fields with equal or lower error and burden.
acceptance_criteria: Synthesis preserves comparator scope and the exact unresolved boundary.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.minimum.0.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is schema theater and validates no language, consent, authority, or effects.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.minimum.0.falsifier: A field or mutation lacks a consumer, counts fail recomputation, or prose exceeds finite routing.
acceptance_criteria: 25 declarations, four targets, 4/6 intake, 2/7 re-contract, and 3/10 plan outcomes reproduce with behavioral non-claims.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.beyond_so.strongest_counterclaim: Simpler dialogue plus confirmation and permissions dominates once burden, latency, privacy, and missed help count.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.beyond_so.falsifier: The plane misses a hard gate, is dominated on the frozen frontier, or fails independent implementation or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Independent implementations meet every hard gate and the frozen Pareto rule across natural, adversarial, multilingual, multi-party, and downstream transfer sets.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.mechanism.strongest_counterclaim: This mechanism is ceremony, lacks a unique consumer, or costs more useful help than it protects. Challenged proposition: Preserve the exact raw request and create a separately versioned interpretation contract; raw conversational context remains recovery evidence and cannot override the accepted contract.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.mechanism.falsifier: It can be removed without changing frozen safety, correction, preservation, or cost outcomes, or an admitted trace bypasses it.
acceptance_criteria: It has an exact consumer, rejects seeded violations, and matched ablation meets frozen benefit and burden gates.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.interface.strongest_counterclaim: This handoff duplicates API permissions and cannot prevent semantic widening. Challenged proposition: Scalable Oversight may supply clarification, disagreement, or review evidence but cannot convert a reviewer or model inference into the principal's authorization.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.interface.falsifier: A consumer accepts a wrong version, widens authority, treats evidence as permission, or loses a material field.
acceptance_criteria: Independent producer and consumer preserve version, authority separation, fields, and rejecting mutations.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.failure_m.falsifier: Prospective stress and matched ablation show no material incidence or consequence.
acceptance_criteria: A preregistered stressor elicits it and the control changes the outcome with coverage, burden, and alternatives visible.

human-intent-as-a-formal-input.formal.in.strongest_counterclaim: This theorem restates Boolean premises and proves no semantic understanding, authentic authority, preservation, or enforcement. Challenged proposition: A compiled intent contract preserves declared constraints and stop conditions.
human-intent-as-a-formal-input.formal.in.falsifier: Lean accepts premises while the encoded conclusion fails, or prose exceeds the finite consequence.
acceptance_criteria: The declaration builds, fixtures reproduce where applicable, and public claims stay within encoded semantics.

non_claims: This sweep establishes claim ownership and test obligations only; it does not establish that the stack understands human intent.
The added obligations are requirements to challenge, not evidence of consent, preference recovery, authority, preservation, useful throughput, safety, or SOTA performance.
Source review, finite Lean routes, deterministic fixtures, and internal adjudication create no support-state movement or external authority.
